questions.question: Which of the following is a purely combinational logic circuit, meaning its output depends only on its current inputs and has no memory or feedback elements?
In a Complex Instruction Set Computer (CISC) architecture, what is the primary role of 'microcode'?
options: To store frequently accessed data for faster retrieval by the CPU.
To translate complex instructions into a sequence of simpler, atomic micro-operations that the hardware can execute.
To manage virtual memory address translations between logical and physical addresses.
To accelerate floating-point arithmetic calculations through dedicated hardware units.
To synchronize data transfer between different clock domains within the processor.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of 'clock gating' in digital IC design?
options: To synchronize data transfer between asynchronous clock domains.
To reduce dynamic power consumption by selectively disabling the clock signal to idle functional blocks or registers.
To ensure all flip-flops within a synchronous domain receive the clock signal at precisely the same time, minimizing clock skew.
To generate multiple clock frequencies from a single input clock for different parts of an SoC.
To prevent clock signal noise from affecting sensitive analog circuits on the same chip.

questions.question: In an out-of-order execution processor, what is the primary architectural technique used to eliminate Write-After-Write (WAW) and Write-After-Read (WAR) data hazards, often by mapping architectural registers to a larger set of physical registers?

As integrated circuit process technology scales down to advanced nodes (e.g., 7nm, 5nm), manufacturing variations across a die and across wafers become more significant. Which advanced timing analysis technique is primarily employed to accurately predict circuit timing under these statistical variations, rather than relying solely on worst-case corner analysis?
